description: §20.1 (Client Router — navigate-soft-nav Wave-1b, S239 re-review finding #5). `navigate(.Soft)` passes ONLY the navigation-type modifier and NO path. The modifier is not a destination, so the compiler fires E-NAV-NO-PATH rather than silently lowering the bare `.Soft` variant into the path slot (which would produce a `_scrml_navigate_soft(".Soft")` bogus-path call).
rationale: §20.1: `navigate(path)` requires a developer path string; the first argument is the destination and `.Hard`/`.Soft` is an optional second-position modifier. A variant-only call has no destination — E-NAV-NO-PATH.
